title: India-Pak drone war fakes — 2026-05-11
narrative: India's PIB and government spokespeople claim Indian strikes destroyed Pakistani military assets at Karachi Port and multiple Pakistani airfields. Pakistan's Defense Minister claims Pakistani drones were not intercepted "to avoid escalation," while Pakistani military PR claims Pakistan shot down Indian aircraft. Both governments cite video evidence as proof of their respective successes.
telemetry: BBC Verify: actively investigating and debunking viral claims from both sides as false
NYT satellite imagery (May 2025): clear but limited damage to Pakistani facilities from Indian strikes — confirmed physical damage, not the scale claimed
Soch Fact Check: video claiming recent drone strike on Islamabad depot appeared online January 10, 2026 — not recent
The Quint: video of drone shootdown linked to LOC actually dates to May 2025 (Operation Sindoor)
Yahoo Fact Check: fake CNN graphic of "India-Pakistan Conflict Statistics" fooled X users
Dawn (May 8, 2026): drone warfare escalation ongoing — conflict continuing past one year
Pakistan on first anniversary of Pahalgam attack: publicly criticized India for "failing to share evidence"
India's PIB fact-check unit debunked Pakistan's own video using the same footage Pakistan released — finding selective cropping
Core contradiction: both governments are publishing selectively cropped or entirely fabricated video evidence while their own fact-check units debunk the other side
analysis:
Official sources say one thing. The evidence says another. This isn't just both sides lie — it's that each side's official fact-checking apparatus is being weaponized against the other's claims using the same method (selective video crop). The physical satellite evidence from NYT puts the real damage at limited scope. The information war is running ahead of the physical reality in both directions.
